zh.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne是Father of Baron le Moigne和Mother of Baron le Moigne的兒子，於大約0990年出生在法國諾門迪曼其素額德瓦爾勒-波斯。他於1023年之前與Mrs. Baron le Moigne在法國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne是Father of Baron le Moigne和Mother of Baron le Moigne的儿子，于大约0990年出生在法国諾門迪曼其素額德瓦爾勒-波斯。他于1023年之前与Mrs. Baron le Moigne在法国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Moigne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from moine ‘monk’, probably applied as an occupational name for a servant at a monastery or an ironic nickname for someone of monkish behavior or appearance. Compare Lemoine .

variants.name: Lemoine
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Pierre, Aime, Clovis, Gaston, Marcel.

French: from Old French moine monk (from Latin monacus), with fused masculine definite article le, probably an occupational name for a servant at a monastery or an ironic nickname for someone of monkish behavior or appearance. Compare Lemoyne and Moine .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Le Moine) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant or altered form Le Moyne) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. — In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, now found e.g. among the descendants of Jean Guyon du Buisson from France (see Guyon ), through Joseph Guyon dit Lemoine.

variants.name: Moinet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Moine   ‘monk’. Compare   Monet  .

variants.name: Moniot
meanings: French:
from Old French moniot, a diminutive of moine ‘monk’ (see Moine ).
from a shortened pet form of Simon or another personal name ending in -mon, such as Aymon (see Aymond ).

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the title of nobility, along the lines of Earl and Duke . The title arose in the Norman feudal system and seems to be of Germanic origin, derived from Old English beorn ‘young warrior’ or a related continental Germanic form. In part the given name, and in particular the variant Barron , may represent the surname derived from the title.

en.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne was born about 0990, in Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandy, France as the son of Father of Baron le Moigne and Mother of Baron le Moigne. He married Mrs. Baron le Moigne before 1023, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne est né environ 0990, à Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandie, France, fils de Father of Baron le Moigne et Mother of Baron le Moigne. Il a épousé Mrs. Baron le Moigne avant 1023, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne wurde um 0990 in Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandie, Frankreich, als Sohn von Father of Baron le Moigne und Mother of Baron le Moigne geboren. Er hat vor 1023 in Frankreich Mrs. Baron le Moigne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne è nato il circa 0990 nel Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandie, Francia come figlio di Father of Baron le Moigne e Mother of Baron le Moigne. Lui sposato Mrs. Baron le Moigne prima del 1023, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandy, Fr: フランスノーマンディマンチェソウルデヴァル-レス-ボイス
ja.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigneは約0990年にフランスノーマンディマンチェソウルデヴァル-レス-ボイスでFather of Baron le MoigneとMother of Baron le Moigneの息子として生まれた。1023年以前にフランスでMrs. Baron le Moigneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne(은)는 대략 0990년 프랑스 노먼디 먼체 소우르데발-레스-보이스에서 Father of Baron le Moigne(와)과 Mother of Baron le Moigne의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1023년 이전 프랑스에서 Mrs. Baron le Moigne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne nasceu aproximadamente 0990, em Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandie, França filho de Father of Baron le Moigne e Mother of Baron le Moigne. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Baron le Moigne antes 1023, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne родился около 0990 г. в местности соурдевал-лес-боис, манч, норманди, Франция. Его родителей звали Father of Baron le Moigne и Mother of Baron le Moigne. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Baron le Moigne перед 1023 г. в местности Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Baron le Moigne nació aproximadamente 0990, en Sourdeval-les-Bois, Manche, Normandie, Francia como hijo de Father of Baron le Moigne y Mother of Baron le Moigne. Se casó con Mrs. Baron le Moigne antes de 1023, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
